channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FBI indicts dozens of people in Philadelphia on drug charges
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than two dozen people have been indicted on drug-related charges as part of a yearslong investigation into a gang in Philadelphia, the Federal Bureau of Investigation announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kamala Harris leaves door open for 2028 presidential run
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kamala Harris isn’t ruling out another run for the White House. In an interview with the BBC posted Saturday, Harris said she expects a woman will be president in the coming years, and it could “possibly” be her. “I am not done,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When David Arsenault takes down a worn, leather-bound 19th-century book from the winding shelves of the Boston Athenaeum, he feels a sense of awe — like he’s handling an artifact in a museum.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Richard Mille watches worth 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' stolen during F1 weekend
item.description: “The group targeted wealthy individuals that were wearing high-end luxury watches,” court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security Administration announces 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment
item.description: Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will rise 2.8 percent in 2026 after the annual cost-of-living adjustment (COLA), the federal government announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inflation hit 3 percent in September as energy prices squeezed households
item.description: Annual inflation rose to 3% in September as a jump in gasoline and energy prices pushed price growth to its highest level since January.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KRON4

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: Law enforcement officers have fired shots at a vehicle that backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area that had been the site of protests against federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man shot 10 times protecting high school cheerleader who died after shooting at bonfire: report
item.description: An Alabama man was shot 10 times while trying to protect a high school cheerleader at a bonfire on early Sunday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey runaway found in Louisiana. Police make human trafficking arrest
item.description: Detectives from the Vice Unit have also launched an investigation into the human trafficking aspects of the case, resulting in further arrests.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mother of missing California girl traveled with her out of state, returned without her
item.description: Melodee's mother, Ashlee Buzzard, has been uncooperative with the investigation, officials said, and has been unable to provide a satisfactory answer for her daughter's location or safety.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Nationwide auto theft ring leads to Bay Area car business: CA AG
item.description: (KRON) -- Forty felony charges were filed Thursday against eight people tied to a nationwide auto theft ring and "complex" altered vehicle identification number scheme. The suspects then sold the 19 stolen cars out of their Bay Area business, announced California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Here’s how indictment says Terry Rozier shared information that paid off for bettors
item.description: NBA guard Terry Rozier was averaging nearly 21 points per game for the Charlotte Hornets as the 2022-23 season neared an end. But during a game against the New Orleans Pelicans, he scored only 5 and played less than 10 minutes before claiming he was hurt.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Entire White House East Wing now demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction
item.description: The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with a ballroom construction, Associated Press photos on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: US strikes 2 more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming Capitol bomb investigators focus on men seen in security video
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming sought to identify two men they believe left a homemade bomb in front of the state Capitol, causing the building's evacuation after a passerby found the object and brought it inside. The bomb, at a time of heightened political angst in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic Sen. Merkley of Oregon speaks for more than 22 hours to protest Trump amid shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Led by Oregon Sen. Jeff Merkley, Democrats seized the Senate floor on Wednesday to protest President Donald Trump's presidency amid the government shutdown and push for Republicans to negotiate with them on expiring health subsidies.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Blind man dragged by federal agents into Oregon ICE facility
item.description: The videos show several federal agents circling around Quinn Haberl before grabbing him by his limbs and carrying him toward the ICE building before dropping him on his head.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Salk, Fox 5

item.title: Israeli settlers beat Palestinian farmers on video as attacks mount during West Bank olive harvest
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — Israeli settlers descended on Palestinian olive harvesters and activists this week in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, beating them with clubs in an attack Palestinian health officials said sent at least one woman to the hospital with serious injuries.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrats look to long term as North Carolina GOP redistricting plan seeks another seat for Trump
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats rallied Tuesday against a new U.S. House map proposed by North Carolina Republicans that would likely reap another GOP seat at President Donald Trump's behest, but the critics acknowledged they'll likely be unable to halt the redraw for now.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here's who has been impacted by the AWS outage
item.description: Scores of popular online platforms experienced problems Monday as Amazon Web Services (AWS) grappled with an outage.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
